bullets.text: Net premiums written grew 23% in Q1 2018, the most Direct auto sales of any quarter in company history, with cost per sale well below the target acquisition cost.
Q1 combined ratio came in sub-90, continuing momentum from a strong 2017 and positioning Progressive well heading into hail season.

Personal Lines vehicle PIFs grew 14% and Property PIFs grew 30%; Progressive crossed 25 million insured vehicles and reached 12 million auto PIFs in February, adding the last million in just eight months.
Agency channel Robinson (auto+home) growth was strong; HQX bundled quoting pilot reached more than 25% of countrywide volume by quarter end, driving direct Robinson growth year over year.

New product model 8.5 launched in Iowa offering more competitive rates for customers with stable prior insurance histories; Smart Haul UBI deployed for Motor Carriers with 2% penetration of eligible applications.

First release of new web servicing environment deployed; Snapshot mobile app collected over 600 million miles of driving data; Customer Relationship Assistant using advanced models launched for CRM personalization.
Raised $1.1 billion via dual-tranche offering: $600 million 30-year senior notes at 4.20% and $500 million perpetual preferred stock at 5.375%; ARX ownership increased to 86.7% for $296 million.
Record Q1 media spend generated record Direct auto sales; HQX incorporated into TV advertising featuring Flo to drive bundled auto and home awareness.
Market volatility returned in Q1 with stocks falling; investment portfolio lost 0.3% as fixed-income and equities contributed equally to the decline; $350 million in equities trimmed to reduce risk.
